LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VALENTIN MORENO-DELGADO'S MOTION TO AMEND IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE FOR THE MOTION AND DEFENDANT WILL NOT BE PREJUDICED ASSUMING THAT THE STIPULATED REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL IS GRANTED. IN THE UNLIKELY EVENT THAT THE TRIAL IS NOT CONTINUED, THE DEFENDANT IS INVITED TO MAKE AN EX PARTE APPLICATION SEEKING RECONSIDERATION OF THIS ORDER. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-03-2016 Continued To Jun-05-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Crown Building Maintenance Cos Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Clarify Definition Of Aggrieved Employee And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Labor Code Allegation
Off Calendar Sep-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ari J. Stiller of Kingsley & Kingsley APC (818-990-8300) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Michael D. Thomas of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart PC (415-536-3431) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE CO's Motion To Strike Or Clarify Definition Of Aggrieved Employee And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Labor Code Allegation is DENIED in its entirety. The discrepancy in the aggrieved party definition between the first amended complaint and the March 2015 letter to LWDA does not as a matter of law require the striking of the first set of allegations sought to be stricken. The portion of the motion seeking to strike the allegations regarding defendant's use of a "partial name" cannot be decided without evidence as to the basis for those allegations. Prevailing party must submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-12-2016 Continued To Mar-13-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Amend
Off Calendar May-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kelsey Szamet of Kingsley & Kingsley, A.P.C. telephone number 818-990-8300 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Valentin Moreno-Delgado.
Michael Thomas of Ogletree Deakins telephone number 415-442-4810 for Defendant Crown Buildings Maintenance.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF VALENTIN MORENO-DELGADO'S Motion To Amend is granted. Plaintiff has shown good cause for the motion and defendant will not be prejudiced assuming that the stipulated request to continue the trial is granted. In the unlikely event that the trial is not continued, the defendant is invited to make an ex parte application seeking reconsideration of this order. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
